title: Process Signals, Abort Trees, Listener Cleanup, and Graceful Shutdown · Inference Brief: Pokedex Ts Shutdown Tests
summary: Every request owns an unrelated controller, SIGINT calls process.exit immediately, event listeners accumulate, pending readline and fetch work survive close, and aborted work prints as a defect. Predict the user decision, runtime value, strict type claim, promise state, API boundary, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex ts shutdown tests increment in composed abort-aware Node session lifecycle. Learners progress throu…
objectives: Test abort before and during question, fetch, cache wait, prefetch, and save plus repeated signal, cleanup order, state, and output.
Stop admission, abort pending questions and fetches, await owned work, persist valid state if policy allows, close readline, then set exit status.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Pokedex Ts Shutdown Tests
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 3040 during an inclusive museum learning kiosk. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove signal-to-abort translation, composed reasons, listener cleanup, ordered close, and state safety; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node or network effects; then change one unknown JSON field, nested member, status, URL, or byte budget and re-run validation. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test abort before and during question, fetch, cache wait, prefetch, and save plus repeated signal, cleanup order, state, and output.
